title: Why Joby Thinks Passenger Air Taxi Service Will Lift Off in 2026
text: Joby Aviation believes its S4 electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft will begin transporting passengers in 2026. The company completed over 850 test flights in 2025, accumulating more than 9,000 miles and conducting demonstrations across multiple countries. According to Chief Test Pilot James Denham, 2025 saw the most extensive and rigorous flight testing in Joby's history. The fleet covered more than 4,900 test points and surpassed 50,000 miles since testing commenced. Public demonstrations took place in Japan, Dubai, and California, with pilots successfully executing transition maneuvers from hover to forward flight. Joby expects to begin FAA Type Inspection Authorization testing in 2026, during which federal aviation administration pilots will operate the aircraft for the first time. The company's aircraft president noted it is possible, depending on where things go, that passenger flights could commence before full FAA certification through a UAE-based qualification program. However, independent analysts project a later timeline, with SMG Consulting indicating insufficient flight hours have been accumulated for certification.
